33.reason: [The recursive approach may revisit the same segment multiple times in different chains, leading to overcounting. Also, it doesn't prevent cycles or reuse of segments, violating the constraint that each segment can be used only once in a chain.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [The recursive approach may count the same segment multiple times in different paths and does not prevent revisiting segments, leading to overcounting. Also, it doesn't ensure that only one continuous chain is followed since multiple matches could exist (though constraints say no three share an endpoint, but recursion still doesn't track visited segments).]
